section_name: Street Lighting in Type a General-law Municipality
chapter_title: Gen. Provisions Relating to Mun. Streets
text:
The governing body of a Type A general-law municipality may:

(1) provide for and regulate the lighting of a street;

(2) create or change lamp districts; or

(3) exclusively regulate or direct the laying or repairing of gas pipes and gas fixtures in a street, alley, sidewalk, or other place.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 311. Gen. Provisions Relating to Mun. Streets
##### § 311.902 — Street Lighting in Type a General-law Municipality

The governing body of a Type A general-law municipality may:

    (1) provide for and regulate the lighting of a street;

    (2) create or change lamp districts; or

    (3) exclusively regulate or direct the laying or repairing of gas pipes and gas fixtures in a street, alley, sidewalk, or other place.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.text: The governing body of a Type A general-law municipality may:
provide for and regulate the lighting of a street;
create or change lamp districts; or
exclusively regulate or direct the laying or repairing of gas pipes and gas fixtures in a street, alley, sidewalk, or other place.
